Subject: **Salary Notification**
Body: This deceptive email often comes from platforms outside of the Cornell environment, masquerading as a "@cornell.edu" address. You might see the sender shown as "Compensation Department <compensation@cornell.edu>". Typically, this message is received following engagement in a misleading student job scheme. It includes a counterfeit paycheck as a PDF attachment. Refrain from attempting to cash these checks and discontinue any communication with the issuer. Refer to advice from Career Services on recognizing employment scam alerts for additional guidance.
original_body: This phish typically originates from non-Cornell email infrastructure and spoofs (or fakes) a Cornell University email address. The sender may appear as "Payroll Services <payroll@cornell.edu>". This email is typically received after a victim has already replied to a student employment scam. The email contains a fake check in the form of a PDF attachment. Do not attempt to deposit the checks and cease all contact with the sender. See  Beware of Job and Internship Scams  from Career Services for more information.
